disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment. Fluids will be drained on-site after collections.

disposal_conditions.dropoff: Drop-off - Plastic Bags, Wrap, & Film Drop off this item for recycling. All bags, wraps, and film should be clean & empty. To find the nearest drop-off location near you, please visit the Plastic Film Recycling's website to enter your zipcode. Do NOT place plastic bags, wraps, and film in residential recycling bins. Special Instructions Deflate pillow before recycling at designated drop-off locations.

disposal_conditions.compost: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

disposal_conditions.compost: Special Instructions Bamboos are a diverse group of mostly evergreen perennial flowering plants, with the Giant Bamboos being the largest members of the grasses family. Very hardy plants, ornamental bamboos are often given as gifts to symbolize good fortune. Bamboo plants are well adjusted to many climates but certain members of the bamboo family can become invasive in the DC area if left unchecked as they can root easily from even semi-brown stalks. For that reason, always place bamboo in a trash can for disposal, they should never be collected as yard waste or placed in a food waste composting bucket.

Special Instructions Bamboos are a diverse group of mostly evergreen perennial flowering plants, with the Giant Bamboos being the largest members of the grasses family. Very hardy plants, ornamental bamboos are often given as gifts to symbolize good fortune. Bamboo plants are well adjusted to many climates but certain members of the bamboo family can become invasive in the DC area if left unchecked as they can root easily from even semi-brown stalks. For that reason, always place bamboo in a trash can for disposal, they should never be collected as yard waste or placed in a food waste composting bucket.

disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment.

disposal_conditions.hazardous: DPW's Special Waste Collection Events - Household Hazardous Waste Drop off this item for safe handling. DPW's offers Special Waste Collection Events every quarter, once on Thursday and Saturday , in conjunction with document shredding and electronics recycling events. View the current drop-off schedule . Battery & Cell Phone Drop-off - The Battery Network Drop off batteries and cellphones for recycling at locations across DC. For the most up-to-date and complete list of drop-off locations, please visit: https://batterynetwork.org/locator/ Learn about DC's Battery Disposal Ban: https://code.dccouncil.gov/us/dc/council/code/sections/8-1041.07
disposal_conditions.dropoff: DPW's Special Waste Collection Events - Household Hazardous Waste Drop off this item for safe handling. DPW's offers Special Waste Collection Events every quarter, once on Thursday and Saturday , in conjunction with document shredding and electronics recycling events. View the current drop-off schedule . Battery & Cell Phone Drop-off - The Battery Network Drop off batteries and cellphones for recycling at locations across DC. For the most up-to-date and complete list of drop-off locations, please visit: https://batterynetwork.org/locator/ Learn about DC's Battery Disposal Ban: https://code.dccouncil.gov/us/dc/council/code/sections/8-1041.07 All batteries must now be disposed of carefully in DC and all batteries may not be disposed of in the trash. Please read about DC's Battery Disposal Ban here: https://code.dccouncil.gov/us/dc/council/code/sections/8-771.09 If you need to dispose of a battery, all types of batteries, please locate a nearby battery disposal options at Call2Recycle; this list of disposal options is consistently updated for your reference: https://www.call2recycle.org/locator/ Please make sure that all rechargeable, lithium ion, NiCad, car batteries, button cell, and batteries with greater than 9 volts are dropped off safely (search for rechargeable batteries for instructions).

disposal_conditions.hazardous: Do not dispose of batteries in the trash or recycling. They must be disposed of carefully and specially. Please read about DC's Battery Disposal Ban here: https://code.dccouncil.gov/us/dc/council/code/sections/8-771.09 To safely dispose of batteries, tape terminals of rechargeable batteries (such as nickel-cadmium/NiCd and lithium ion), button cell batteries, and batteries greater than 9 volts. These batteries could cause fires or are considered hazardous waste. Do not place in a trash or a recycling bin. If you need to dispose of any type of battery in DC, please locate a nearby battery disposal option at The Battery Network; this list of disposal options is consistently updated for your reference: https://batterynetwork.org/locator/ DPW's Special Waste Collection Events - Household Hazardous Waste Drop off this item for safe handling. disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment. Consider donating, repairing, or selling if possible. Check out the District's reuse directory .

disposal_conditions.compost: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

disposal_conditions.compost: Yard Waste DPW collects yard waste from residences that receive DPW’s trash and recycling collection services. Yard waste consists of any one or a combination of the following: fallen leaves, grass clippings, plant trimmings, weeds, bulbs, branches, twigs, pinecones, and uprooted plants. Yard waste can also include bundled branches and tree limbs of no more than 4 inches in diameter and 4-feet long and tied together with natural twine into bundle no longer than 4-foot long. DPW will not collect yard waste that includes branches and tree limbs greater than 4 inches in diameter or 4 feet in length, and stumps, dirt, stones, rocks, broken concrete, and broken pottery flowerpots. Yard waste DOES NOT include large tree limbs (>4 inches in diameter or >4 feet in length, stumps, dirt, stones, rocks, broken concrete, and broken pottery flower pots. Please visit our website for more information . Yard waste collection is now year-round. Residents can call 311 to make an appointment for yard waste collection. Yard waste collection is now year-round. Residents can call 311 to make an appointment for yard waste collection. Residents can also visit www.311.dc.gov to make an appointment or download and schedule an appointment using the 311 mobile app available at the Google Play and Apple App stores. Resident may also drop off their yard waste at the Fort Totten Transfer Station Tuesday – Friday 10:00 am -2:00 pm and Saturday 7:00 am - 2:00 pm DPW only collects yard waste in paper bags, which is unlike previous years when plastic bags were acceptable for collection. Beginning August 16, 2021, yard waste will only be collected in paper bags. DPW will collect up to 20 paper bags of yard waste from residences that receive DPW’s trash and recycling collection services. Special Instructions There are two primary types of brooms, plastic brooms (with synthetic bristles made of a plastic material) and natural brooms (with natural bristles typically made of a natural fiber like thatch or grass). When a plastic broom has met the end of its useful life, it should be placed in a trash bin or trash dumpster for disposal but never down a trash chute. Broomsticks of all sizes can get lodged in trash chutes and can cause trash chutes to become clogged, which may lead to costly repairs and unsafe conditions. If the broom will not fit in a trash bin, sometimes the head of the broom can be removed from the stick for easier disposal. Some natural brooms that have been used only for sweeping up dirt and household dust are compostable if the natural fiber bristles can break off easily. If the broom has been used to cleanup up chemicals, it should be placed in the trash. Never place any metal or plastic pieces of a broom in a recycling bin or compost pile.

disposal_conditions.dropoff: There is no curbside collection for this electronic waste item. DPW's Special Waste Collection Events - Electronic Waste Drop off this item for recycling. DPW's offers Special Waste Collection Events every quarter, once on Thursday and Saturday , in conjunction with the household hazardous waste and document shredding events. View the current drop-off schedule . Drop-off - eCYCLE DC Computers Drop off this item for recycling. Manufacturers of certain electronics (televisions, computers, and related equipment) are required to collect and properly manage electronic waste. Note that some manufacturer stewardship programs may charge a fee or have limitations on the types of products they accept, check drop off location for more details. Please also visit eCYCLE DC website for the most up-to-date information . Drop-Off at Best Buy - Electronics, Appliances and Fitness Equipment Recycling Can recycle up to three items per household per day for free. Some items are Haul-a-way for a fee. See web site for details. https://www.bestbuy.com/site/services/recycling/pcmcat149900050025.c?id=pcmcat149900050025

disposal_conditions.compost: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment. Consider donating, repairing, or selling if possible. Visit reuse.dc.gov to learn where you can donate or repair this item and more!

Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment. Consider donating or selling this item if still usable. Visit reuse.dc.gov to learn where you can donate this item and more!

disposal_conditions.compost: Holiday Tree Collection For the 2019-2020 Holiday Season - The Department of Public Works will collect holiday trees and greenery for composting, by appointment, between December 27 – January 10. The collection service is provided solely to residents in DPW-serviced households. Residents must call 311 to make an appointment before January 10 to secure this service. For the most up-to-date information regarding holiday tree collection, please visit our website . Special Instructions All residents may drop off holiday trees and greenery for composting through January 31 Monday - Saturday 9 AM to 4 PM at one of the drop off locations listed below: Benning Road Transfer Station , 3200 Benning Road, NE: 9 AM-4 PM Fort Totten Transfer Station , 4900 Bates Road, NE: 9 AM-4 PM Bryant Street Sweep Shop , 201 Bryant Street, NW: 9 AM-4 PM South Capitol Street Graffiti Shop , 2700 South Capitol Street, SE 9 AM-4 PM

disposal_conditions.recycling: Drop-off - eCYCLE DC Computers Drop off this item for recycling. Manufacturers of certain electronics (televisions, computers, and related equipment) are required to collect and properly manage electronic waste. Note that some manufacturer stewardship programs may charge a fee or have limitations on the types of products they accept, check drop off location for more details. Please also visit eCYCLE DC website for the most up-to-date information .

disposal_conditions.compost: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

disposal_conditions.dropoff: Compostable bin liners are accepted a food waste drop off if they are made of compostable materials including paper (like a brown paper lunch sack or a brown paper shopping bag) and only BPI-certified compostable plastic. You can tell that a compostable bag is acceptable in the food waste drop-off bins because it will have the BPI-certified compostable logo. If a "compostable" plastic bag does not have the BPI-certified compostable logo on it, than it should be put into the trash. Never put a plastic bag, regardless of whether it is compostable or not, in the recycling bin as it can become tangled and damage recycling equipment. Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

disposal_conditions.compost: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

Cardboard that is too large to fit inside your container must be bundled, tied and placed alongside the recycling container. Cardboard boxes cannot be any longer than 4 feet in all direction for proper disposal.

disposal_conditions.dropoff: DPW's Special Waste Collection Events - Document Shredding Drop off this item for shredding at DPW's Special Waste Collection Events.Residents may bring up to five, medium-size boxes (approximately 20” x 14” x 14”) of personal documents to be shredded. Only paper (staples, paper and binder clips on the paper are okay) and credit cards will be accepted. DPW's offers Special Waste Collection Events every quarter, once on Thursday and Saturday , in conjunction with the household hazardous waste and electronics recycling events. View the current drop-off schedule . Business or commercial material is NOT accepted. Any metal or glass items. (Please check boxes before you bring them) No non-personal documents that can be recycled through your recycling program. All staples, rubber bands, paper clips, even black “alligator clips” are acceptable at document shredding events.
disposal_conditions.hazardous: DPW's Special Waste Collection Events - Document Shredding Drop off this item for shredding at DPW's Special Waste Collection Events.Residents may bring up to five, medium-size boxes (approximately 20” x 14” x 14”) of personal documents to be shredded. Only paper (staples, paper and binder clips on the paper are okay) and credit cards will be accepted. DPW's offers Special Waste Collection Events every quarter, once on Thursday and Saturday , in conjunction with the household hazardous waste and electronics recycling events. View the current drop-off schedule . Business or commercial material is NOT accepted. Any metal or glass items. (Please check boxes before you bring them) No non-personal documents that can be recycled through your recycling program. Effective January 1, 2017, the same entities must now only use recyclable or compostable food service ware. In addition to foam, items now banned include mixed materials like paper bags with a plastic window and paper-lined foil sandwich wraps. If you suspect a food service entity is using foam or mixed materials in violation of the District’s Food Service Ware Requirements, please submit a tip using 311 ( 311.dc.gov ) or on the DOEE website ( doee.dc.gov/node/1087982 ).

disposal_conditions.dropoff: Do not place deceased pets include in your household trash. Read special instructions below.
disposal_conditions.special: DPW trash containers are intended for everyday household waste not for the disposal of animals. In the interest of public health and safety, DPW collects animals such as wildlife or roadkill who have died on public property (i.e., sidewalks, streets and alleys). DPW does not go onto private property to remove a dead animal. If residents or visitors see a dead animal in a public space, they may contact 311 to open a service request to have it removed. Per DC Health, if you’ve experienced the loss of a pet, there are several options for a proper and respectful removal. Individuals should first contact their veterinary practice for guidance, as all veterinary facilities in the District are required to have a rendering service available. Alternatively, individuals may choose to contact a private service that can retrieve the animal from their home. To prepare your deceased pet for removal, the animal’s body should be placed in a properly sealed plastic bag to contain any bodily fluids and stored in a cool location to slow natural decomposition. You may also contact Brandywine Valley SPCA at 202-888-PETS to learn more about their available pet aftercare services. These are offered at a cost, and their team is here to support you during this time. To learn more about animal services, please contact DC Health’s Animal Services Program by visiting https://dchealth.dc.gov/animal-services or calling 202-888-PETS.

disposal_conditions.dropoff: Drop off at Fort Totten Transfer Station - Bulk, Trash, Recycling Drop off item for recycling.
disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment.

disposal_conditions.dropoff: There is no curbside collection for this electronic waste item. DPW's Special Waste Collection Events - Electronic Waste Drop off this item for recycling. DPW's offers Special Waste Collection Events every quarter, once on Thursday and Saturday , in conjunction with the household hazardous waste and document shredding events. View the current drop-off schedule . Drop-off - eCYCLE DC Computers Drop off this item for recycling. Manufacturers of certain electronics (televisions, computers, and related equipment) are required to collect and properly manage electronic waste. Note that some manufacturer stewardship programs may charge a fee or have limitations on the types of products they accept, check drop off location for more details. Please also visit eCYCLE DC website for the most up-to-date information . Drop-Off at Best Buy - Electronics, Appliances and Fitness Equipment Recycling Can recycle up to three items per household per day for free. Some items are Haul-a-way for a fee. See web site for details. https://www.bestbuy.com/site/services/recycling/pcmcat149900050025.c?id=pcmcat149900050025

Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. disposal_conditions.donate: Extra edible food that can be donated should be donated before trashing it. But if it cannot be donated, consider composting it at one of DC's Food Waste Drop-off options. Donate - Edible Food Edible food that is still fresh, not expired, uneaten and can be donated should be donated to feed people and animals. There are several organizations that can readily accept edible food for donation from individuals in DC. Consider reaching out to these organizations listed below. (Note: The following list is not complete. Other organizations, such as churches, grocers, community centers, and shelters occasionally host food drives. Look for food drives and food pantries near you, too). Special Instructions Edible food that is still fresh, not expired, uneaten, and able to be donated should be donated to feed people or animals before you compost or trash it. If you cannot donate it for whatever reason, and you want to compost it, it must be removed from its packaging (no exceptions) if you drop it off at a DC DPW food waste drop-off bin.
disposal_conditions.compost: Extra edible food that can be donated should be donated before trashing it. But if it cannot be donated, consider composting it at one of DC's Food Waste Drop-off options. Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website . Special Instructions Edible food that is still fresh, not expired, uneaten, and able to be donated should be donated to feed people or animals before you compost or trash it. If you cannot donate it for whatever reason, and you want to compost it, it must be removed from its packaging (no exceptions) if you drop it off at a DC DPW food waste drop-off bin.

For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website . Extra edible food that can be donated should be donated before trashing it. disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment.

disposal_conditions.special: Place road flares in a plastic zipper bag and then zip the bag firmly closed. Place the zipped plastic bag inside of a trash bag, and store it somewhere safe. Because flares may pose a safety hazard in addition to an environmental hazard, it is important to drop them off as soon as possible. Make sure that you inform the recipients that you are dropping off flares, tell them whether or not the flares have been used, and tell them the procedure that you used for bagging up the flares

disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment. Special Instructions Rugs and carpet must be rolled and tied for bulk trash collection. Rugs and carpet must be rolled and tied for bulk trash collection.

disposal_conditions.compost: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

disposal_conditions.compost: Special Instructions Beginning January 1, 2016, the District’s ban on foam containers prohibits businesses or organizations that sell or provide food from using foam food service ware (cups, plates, bowls, take-out containers, etc.). Effective January 1, 2017, the same entities must now only use recyclable or compostable food service ware. In addition to foam, items now banned include mixed materials like paper bags with a plastic window and paper-lined foil sandwich wraps. If you suspect a food service entity is using foam or mixed materials in violation of the District’s Food Service Ware Requirements, please submit a tip using 311 ( 311.dc.gov ) or on the DOEE website ( doee.dc.gov/node/1087982 ).

disposal_conditions.compost: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website . Special Instructions Food scraps and food-soiled paper are accepted for composting through the DPW's Food Waste Drop-Off program (residents only, not for business use). For a full list of accepted items, please visit the website . If you reside in a single-family home or a building with three residential units or fewer, and you receive trash and recycling services from DC DPW, you may sign up for future curbside food waste collection. Learn more and express your interest by visiting: https://zerowaste.dc.gov/curbsidecomposting . Learn about DC's Commercial Food Waste Diversion requirements for certain types of food waste generators: https://code.dccouncil.gov/us/dc/council/code/sections/8-1031.03a

Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

Trash Put this item in your trash. Trash should be in a plastic bag inside green trash bins. Place trash bin out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before an no later than 6 am on your collection day. Bins should be removed from public space by 8 pm on your collection day.

Special Instructions Food scraps and food-soiled paper are accepted for composting through the DPW's Food Waste Drop-Off program (residents only, not for business use). For a full list of accepted items, please visit the website . If you reside in a single-family home or a building with three residential units or fewer, and you receive trash and recycling services from DC DPW, you may sign up for future curbside food waste collection. Learn more and express your interest by visiting: https://zerowaste.dc.gov/curbsidecomposting . Learn about DC's Commercial Food Waste Diversion requirements for certain types of food waste generators: https://code.dccouncil.gov/us/dc/council/code/sections/8-1031.03a

disposal_conditions.donate: Repair - Appliances Consider repairing this item. Visit reuse.dc.gov to learn where you can repair this item and more! You may also bring your items in need of repair to the free community repair events offered by Fix-It DC .
disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment. Drain all liquids and remove all doors.

items.explanation: Drain all liquids and remove all doors.

disposal_conditions.compost: Grasscycling Please "grasscycle" by leaving grass clippings on your lawn after mowing. For more information on grasscycling, visit DPW's website: https://dpw.dc.gov/page/grasscycling Yard Waste DPW collects yard waste from residences that receive DPW’s trash and recycling collection services. Yard waste consists of any one or a combination of the following: fallen leaves, grass clippings, plant trimmings, weeds, bulbs, branches, twigs, pinecones, and uprooted plants. Yard waste can also include bundled branches and tree limbs of no more than 4 inches in diameter and 4-feet long and tied together with natural twine into bundle no longer than 4-foot long. DPW will not collect yard waste that includes branches and tree limbs greater than 4 inches in diameter or 4 feet in length, and stumps, dirt, stones, rocks, broken concrete, and broken pottery flowerpots. Yard waste DOES NOT include large tree limbs (>4 inches in diameter or >4 feet in length, stumps, dirt, stones, rocks, broken concrete, and broken pottery flower pots. Please visit our website for more information . Yard waste collection is now year-round. Residents can call 311 to make an appointment for yard waste collection. Yard waste collection is now year-round. Residents can call 311 to make an appointment for yard waste collection. Residents can also visit www.311.dc.gov to make an appointment or download and schedule an appointment using the 311 mobile app available at the Google Play and Apple App stores. Resident may also drop off their yard waste at the Fort Totten Transfer Station Tuesday – Friday 10:00 am -2:00 pm and Saturday 7:00 am - 2:00 pm DPW only collects yard waste in paper bags, which is unlike previous years when plastic bags were acceptable for collection. Beginning August 16, 2021, yard waste will only be collected in paper bags. DPW will collect up to 20 paper bags of yard waste from residences that receive DPW’s trash and recycling collection services.

Resident may also drop off their yard waste at the Fort Totten Transfer Station Tuesday – Friday 10:00 am -2:00 pm and Saturday 7:00 am - 2:00 pm DPW only collects yard waste in paper bags, which is unlike previous years when plastic bags were acceptable for collection. Beginning August 16, 2021, yard waste will only be collected in paper bags. DPW will collect up to 20 paper bags of yard waste from residences that receive DPW’s trash and recycling collection services.

Grasscycling Please "grasscycle" by leaving grass clippings on your lawn after mowing. For more information on grasscycling, visit DPW's website: https://dpw.dc.gov/page/grasscycling

Yard Waste DPW collects yard waste from residences that receive DPW’s trash and recycling collection services. Yard waste consists of any one or a combination of the following: fallen leaves, grass clippings, plant trimmings, weeds, bulbs, branches, twigs, pinecones, and uprooted plants. Yard waste can also include bundled branches and tree limbs of no more than 4 inches in diameter and 4-feet long and tied together with natural twine into bundle no longer than 4-foot long. DPW will not collect yard waste that includes branches and tree limbs greater than 4 inches in diameter or 4 feet in length, and stumps, dirt, stones, rocks, broken concrete, and broken pottery flowerpots. Yard waste DOES NOT include large tree limbs (>4 inches in diameter or >4 feet in length, stumps, dirt, stones, rocks, broken concrete, and broken pottery flower pots. Please visit our website for more information . Yard waste collection is now year-round. Residents can call 311 to make an appointment for yard waste collection. Resident may also drop off their yard waste at the Fort Totten Transfer Station Tuesday – Friday 10:00 am -2:00 pm and Saturday 7:00 am - 2:00 pm DPW only collects yard waste in paper bags, which is unlike previous years when plastic bags were acceptable for collection. Beginning August 16, 2021, yard waste will only be collected in paper bags. DPW will collect up to 20 paper bags of yard waste from residences that receive DPW’s trash and recycling collection services.

disposal_conditions.dropoff: Recycle Paper Cards Only. Cards with glitter and metallic are not recyclable. Place in trash. Musical cards are neither trash or recyclable. The battery can cause fires and harm workers. Please drop off. Battery & Cell Phone Drop-off - The Battery Network Drop off batteries and cellphones for recycling at locations across DC. For the most up-to-date and complete list of drop-off locations, please visit: https://batterynetwork.org/locator/ Learn about DC's Battery Disposal Ban: https://code.dccouncil.gov/us/dc/council/code/sections/8-1041.07 Special Instructions Greeting cards and envelopes made of paper materials can be put in the recycling bin. Cards with glitter, metallic accents or plastic should be placed in the trash. Photo paper greeting cards also cannot be recycled. Musical Greeting Cards are not accepted in Trash or Recycling. Please remove the battery and drop off at Fort Totten for hazardous waste or retail battery drop off. Then recycle the paper.

Please also visit eCYCLE DC website for the most up-to-date information . Drop-Off at Best Buy - Electronics, Appliances and Fitness Equipment Recycling Can recycle up to three items per household per day for free. Some items are Haul-a-way for a fee. disposal_conditions.compost: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

disposal_conditions.donate: Donate or sell if in working condition. Check out the District's reuse directory . Otherwise, trash if the houseware is not mostly metal or rigid plastic. If items are mostly metal, they can be taken to Fort Totten for scrap metal recycling. Donate - Household Items Donate, drop off, or arrange a pickup for this item. Visit reuse.dc.gov to learn where you can donate this item and more!
disposal_conditions.dropoff: Donate - Household Items Donate, drop off, or arrange a pickup for this item. Visit reuse.dc.gov to learn where you can donate this item and more! Drop off at Fort Totten Transfer Station - Bulk, Trash, Recycling Drop off item for recycling. Donate or sell if in working condition. Check out the District's reuse directory . Otherwise, trash if the houseware is not mostly metal or rigid plastic. If items are mostly metal, they can be taken to Fort Totten for scrap metal recycling.
disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment.
disposal_conditions.garbage: Trash Put this item in your trash. Trash should be in a plastic bag inside green trash bins. Place trash bin out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before an no later than 6 am on your collection day. Bins should be removed from public space by 8 pm on your collection day.

disposal_conditions.special: Empty Flexible Ice Packs If your ice pack has a flexible plastic shell, thaw the ice pack and then cut it open and squeeze the gel into the garbage. Afterward, rinse out the plastic film, dry it and recycle it with plastic bags . Do not pour ice pack contents down the drain . If you accidentally puncture an ice pack or cut it open to empty it, dispose of the mixture in the trash. It can clog sinks and toilets.

disposal_conditions.special: Before placing knives in the trash, it's crucial to package them securely to prevent injury to waste collection workers. A good method is to wrap the knife in several layers of cardboard and secure it tightly with tape, then place it in a sealed container (e.g., an empty laundry detergent bottle or bleach bottle) before putting it in your trash bin.

disposal_conditions.dropoff: There is no curbside collection for this electronic waste item. DPW's Special Waste Collection Events - Electronic Waste Drop off this item for recycling. DPW's offers Special Waste Collection Events every quarter, once on Thursday and Saturday , in conjunction with the household hazardous waste and document shredding events. View the current drop-off schedule . Drop-off - eCYCLE DC Computers Drop off this item for recycling. Manufacturers of certain electronics (televisions, computers, and related equipment) are required to collect and properly manage electronic waste. Note that some manufacturer stewardship programs may charge a fee or have limitations on the types of products they accept, check drop off location for more details. Please also visit eCYCLE DC website for the most up-to-date information . Drop-Off at Best Buy - Electronics, Appliances and Fitness Equipment Recycling Can recycle up to three items per household per day for free. Some items are Haul-a-way for a fee. See web site for details. https://www.bestbuy.com/site/services/recycling/pcmcat149900050025.c?id=pcmcat149900050025

disposal_conditions.compost: Leaf Collection DPW will collect leaves at least twice from each residential neighborhood by “vacuuming” loose leaves residents rake into their treebox(es). Loose leaves will be sent for composting. In neighborhoods with alley trash/recycling collections, bagged leaves may be placed where trash and recycling are collected. These leaves will be collected with the trash as space in the truck permits. If you are bagging your leaves, please use paper bags. Rake leaves into the treebox space the Sunday before your street’s collection weeks. For the most up-to-date information regarding leaf collection, please visit our website .

disposal_conditions.donate: Repair - Luggage Consider repairing this item. Visit reuse.dc.gov to find out where you can repair this item and more! You may also bring your items in need of repair to the free community repair events offered by Fix-It DC .
disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment.

Drop-off - Medications Drop off this item for safe disposal. Drop-off - National Drug Take-Back Day Drop off this item for safe disposal. The National Prescription Drug Take Back Day aims to provide a safe, convenient, and responsible means of disposing of prescription drugs, get more information . Special Instructions If you cannot make it to a drop-off location or event, mix crushed pills or liquid with dirt, cat litter, or used coffee grounds, then put in the trash. Recycle empty bottle after removing personal information and label from the bottle.

disposal_conditions.hazardous: There is no curbside collection for this household hazardous waste. Special Instructions Place in larger container with a tight fitting lid. Place kitty litter or oil-absorbent matter around the product to protect it from breaking or sudden shocks. Clearly label storage container as "Mercury - DO NOT OPEN." If you must wait for a hazardous waste collection day, store products safely in their original containers with the labels intact, and keep them out of reach of children and pets. Transport containers to a household hazardous collection center in a cardboard box. Secure the containers so that they do not tip over, and to minimize shifting or sliding during sudden stops or turns. Transport containers in the back of a pick-up truck or in your car's trunk. If you must transport in the passenger compartment, make sure there is adequate ventilation. Place in larger container with a tight fitting lid. disposal_conditions.compost: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

disposal_conditions.special: The Anacostia River Clean Up and Protection Act of 2009 – commonly known as the “ Bag Law ” – requires that District businesses that sell food or alcohol charge a $0.05 fee for each paper and plastic bag distributed with any purchase, with certain exemptions. If you suspect that a business is in violation of the District's Bag Law, please provide us with details about the business and the suspected violation. Please submit a tip using 311 ( 311.dc.gov ) or on the DOEE website ( doee.dc.gov/baglawtip ).

items.explanation: The Anacostia River Clean Up and Protection Act of 2009 – commonly known as the “ Bag Law ” – requires that District businesses that sell food or alcohol charge a $0.05 fee for each paper and plastic bag distributed with any purchase, with certain exemptions. If you suspect that a business is in violation of the District's Bag Law, please provide us with details about the business and the suspected violation. Please submit a tip using 311 ( 311.dc.gov ) or on the DOEE website ( doee.dc.gov/baglawtip ).

Special Instructions The Anacostia River Clean Up and Protection Act of 2009 – commonly known as the “ Bag Law ” – requires that District businesses that sell food or alcohol charge a $0.05 fee for each paper and plastic bag distributed with any purchase, with certain exemptions. If you suspect that a business is in violation of the District's Bag Law, please provide us with details about the business and the suspected violation. Please submit a tip using 311 ( 311.dc.gov ) or on the DOEE website ( doee.dc.gov/baglawtip ).

disposal_conditions.compost: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website . Special Instructions Uncooked pasta is accepted for DPW's Food Waste Drop-Off program, however cooked pasta go in trash. Composting is not required at this time, however some commercial properties and businesses are participating voluntarily. Cooked and uncooked pasta may also be composted at certain properties.

The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

disposal_conditions.dropoff: DPW's Special Waste Collection Events - Document Shredding Drop off this item for shredding at DPW's Special Waste Collection Events.Residents may bring up to five, medium-size boxes (approximately 20” x 14” x 14”) of personal documents to be shredded. Only paper (staples, paper and binder clips on the paper are okay) and credit cards will be accepted. DPW's offers Special Waste Collection Events every quarter, once on Thursday and Saturday , in conjunction with the household hazardous waste and electronics recycling events. View the current drop-off schedule . Business or commercial material is NOT accepted. Any metal or glass items. (Please check boxes before you bring them) No non-personal documents that can be recycled through your recycling program. All staples, rubber bands, paper clips, even black “alligator clips” are acceptable at document shredding events.

disposal_conditions.dropoff: DPW's Special Waste Collection Events - Document Shredding Drop off this item for shredding at DPW's Special Waste Collection Events.Residents may bring up to five, medium-size boxes (approximately 20” x 14” x 14”) of personal documents to be shredded. Only paper (staples, paper and binder clips on the paper are okay) and credit cards will be accepted. DPW's offers Special Waste Collection Events every quarter, once on Thursday and Saturday , in conjunction with the household hazardous waste and electronics recycling events. View the current drop-off schedule . Business or commercial material is NOT accepted. Any metal or glass items. (Please check boxes before you bring them) No non-personal documents that can be recycled through your recycling program. All staples, rubber bands, paper clips, even black “alligator clips” are acceptable at document shredding events.

disposal_conditions.compost: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website . Special Instructions Pizza crusts are accepted for DPW's Food Waste Drop-Off program, but rest of the pizza goes in trash.

disposal_conditions.garbage: Trash Put this item in your trash. Trash should be in a plastic bag inside green trash bins. Place trash bin out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before an no later than 6 am on your collection day. Bins should be removed from public space by 8 pm on your collection day.
disposal_conditions.special: The Anacostia River Clean Up and Protection Act of 2009 – commonly known as the “ Bag Law ” – requires that District businesses that sell food or alcohol charge a $0.05 fee for each paper and plastic bag distributed with any purchase, with certain exemptions. If you suspect that a business is in violation of the District's Bag Law, please provide us with details about the business and the suspected violation. Please submit a tip using 311 ( 311.dc.gov ) or on the DOEE website ( doee.dc.gov/baglawtip ).

disposal_conditions.dropoff: Drop off at Fort Totten Transfer Station - Bulk, Trash, Recycling Drop off item for recycling. Consider selling, repairing, or donating this item if possible before dropping off. Check out the District's reuse directory .
disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment.

disposal_conditions.compost: Special Instructions Beginning January 1, 2019, businesses and organizations that sell or serve food or beverages in the District must no longer provide single-use plastic straws and stirrers to consumers.* If you observe a food service entity providing single-use plastic straws or stirrers in violation of the District’s compostable and recyclable food service ware requirements, please submit a tip using 311 (311.dc.gov) or on the DOEE website ( https://doee.dc.gov/node/1087982 ). *Pursuant to the Americans with Disabilities Act and the DC Human Rights Act, some customers may request single-use plastic straws to consume food and beverages. Regulated entities must keep a stock of plastic straws available to meet these needs and remain compliant.

disposal_conditions.donate: Repair - Appliances Consider repairing this item. Visit reuse.dc.gov to learn where you can repair this item and more! You may also bring your items in need of repair to the free community repair events offered by Fix-It DC .
disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment.

disposal_conditions.compost: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

disposal_conditions.donate: Repair - Appliances Consider repairing this item. Visit reuse.dc.gov to learn where you can repair this item and more! You may also bring your items in need of repair to the free community repair events offered by Fix-It DC .
disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment. Remove all doors.

A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment.

disposal_conditions.hazardous: Trash - Sharps The improper disposal of contaminated sharps is a serious safety concern for garbage collectors and landfill workers. If improperly thrown in trash bags along with regular trash, these sharps can puncture the bags and cause injury. Check with your doctor, clinic, or hospital to see about a needle or “sharps” disposal program at their office. Prevent injury, illness, and pollution by following these simple steps to dispose of sharp needles and contaminated materials used when administering home health care. We want to keep you, your family, and trash collection crews safe. Place needles, syringes, lancets and other contaminated sharps in any puncture-resistant, resealable, disposable household container (examples include an empty bleach bottle, laundry detergent bottle, or metal coffee can). We recommend choosing a container that has a small opening so no one can stick his or her hand into it. Using a RED marker, write on the container -- “BIOHAZARD—DO NOT RECYCLE.” The color Red is required to indicate a biohazard waste. Used needles and other contaminated sharps are NOT recyclable. Do not recap, purposely bend, break, or otherwise manipulate needles before inserting them into the disposal container. Drop all parts into the container. Once your container is full of used needles, fill the container with one part bleach solution and ten parts of water. Allow solution to soak for 20 minutes; this action will sterilize your used sharps. Then, pour the solution into the sink and seal the cap with tape before placing the disposal container into the garbage. Use heavy-duty tape (such as duct tape). Dispose of the container in your regular trash but do NOT use glass or clear plastic containers. We recommend you place any soiled bandages, disposable sheets and medical gloves separately in securely fastened plastic bags before placing them along with your other trash. Never share used needles. See this flyer: Recommended Steps for the Safe Disposal of Needles or “Sharps” in the District of Columbia

disposal_conditions.dropoff: DPW's Special Waste Collection Events - Document Shredding Drop off this item for shredding at DPW's Special Waste Collection Events.Residents may bring up to five, medium-size boxes (approximately 20” x 14” x 14”) of personal documents to be shredded. Only paper (staples, paper and binder clips on the paper are okay) and credit cards will be accepted. DPW's offers Special Waste Collection Events every quarter, once on Thursday and Saturday , in conjunction with the household hazardous waste and electronics recycling events. View the current drop-off schedule . Business or commercial material is NOT accepted. Any metal or glass items. (Please check boxes before you bring them) No non-personal documents that can be recycled through your recycling program. All staples, rubber bands, paper clips, even black “alligator clips” are acceptable at document shredding events.

disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment.

disposal_conditions.compost: Yard Waste DPW collects yard waste from residences that receive DPW’s trash and recycling collection services. Yard waste consists of any one or a combination of the following: fallen leaves, grass clippings, plant trimmings, weeds, bulbs, branches, twigs, pinecones, and uprooted plants. Yard waste can also include bundled branches and tree limbs of no more than 4 inches in diameter and 4-feet long and tied together with natural twine into bundle no longer than 4-foot long. DPW will not collect yard waste that includes branches and tree limbs greater than 4 inches in diameter or 4 feet in length, and stumps, dirt, stones, rocks, broken concrete, and broken pottery flowerpots. Yard waste DOES NOT include large tree limbs (>4 inches in diameter or >4 feet in length, stumps, dirt, stones, rocks, broken concrete, and broken pottery flower pots. Please visit our website for more information . Yard waste collection is now year-round. Residents can call 311 to make an appointment for yard waste collection. Yard waste collection is now year-round. Residents can call 311 to make an appointment for yard waste collection. Residents can also visit www.311.dc.gov to make an appointment or download and schedule an appointment using the 311 mobile app available at the Google Play and Apple App stores. Resident may also drop off their yard waste at the Fort Totten Transfer Station Tuesday – Friday 10:00 am -2:00 pm and Saturday 7:00 am - 2:00 pm DPW only collects yard waste in paper bags, which is unlike previous years when plastic bags were acceptable for collection. Beginning August 16, 2021, yard waste will only be collected in paper bags. DPW will collect up to 20 paper bags of yard waste from residences that receive DPW’s trash and recycling collection services.

disposal_conditions.compost: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .
disposal_conditions.dropoff: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. disposal_conditions.dropoff: Drop-off - Construction & Demolition Debris Drop off this item for recycling. Drop off locations may vary in what they accept, check Depot description first. Visit the Builders Recycling Guide for drop-off locations .
disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment.

disposal_conditions.compost: Yard Waste DPW collects yard waste from residences that receive DPW’s trash and recycling collection services. Yard waste consists of any one or a combination of the following: fallen leaves, grass clippings, plant trimmings, weeds, bulbs, branches, twigs, pinecones, and uprooted plants. Yard waste can also include bundled branches and tree limbs of no more than 4 inches in diameter and 4-feet long and tied together with natural twine into bundle no longer than 4-foot long. DPW will not collect yard waste that includes branches and tree limbs greater than 4 inches in diameter or 4 feet in length, and stumps, dirt, stones, rocks, broken concrete, and broken pottery flowerpots. Yard waste DOES NOT include large tree limbs (>4 inches in diameter or >4 feet in length, stumps, dirt, stones, rocks, broken concrete, and broken pottery flower pots. Please visit our website for more information . Yard waste collection is now year-round. Residents can call 311 to make an appointment for yard waste collection. Yard waste collection is now year-round. Residents can call 311 to make an appointment for yard waste collection. Residents can also visit www.311.dc.gov to make an appointment or download and schedule an appointment using the 311 mobile app available at the Google Play and Apple App stores. Resident may also drop off their yard waste at the Fort Totten Transfer Station Tuesday – Friday 10:00 am -2:00 pm and Saturday 7:00 am - 2:00 pm DPW only collects yard waste in paper bags, which is unlike previous years when plastic bags were acceptable for collection. Beginning August 16, 2021, yard waste will only be collected in paper bags. DPW will collect up to 20 paper bags of yard waste from residences that receive DPW’s trash and recycling collection services.

disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment. Consider selling, repairing, or donating this item if possible before dropping off. Check out the District's reuse directory .

disposal_conditions.compost: Drop-off - Food Waste District residents have 40+ food waste drop-off options in DC. Residents may drop-off food waste at up to 13 weekend drop-off events at designated farmers' markets and community locations or at 30+ Food Waste Smart Bins in all eight wards free of charge. The food waste will be taken to a local composting facility where it will be turned into compost, an environmentally friendly-soil amendment. Remove all food from packaging before dropping it off. For the most up-to-date locations and market times, please visit our website .

disposal_conditions.special: Bulk Trash Collection This item is accepted for bulk waste collection. Bulk trash collection is a service available only to residents who are regularly serviced by DPW. Bulk items are picked up at the same place you set out your regular trash. Up to seven (7) items may be collected at one time. Customers with more than seven items may request additional appointments. Call the Mayor's Citywide Call Center at 311 to make an appointment, which is usually available within 10 to 14 days of your call. You can also visit www.311.dc.gov . Place your bulk items out no earlier than 6:30 pm the day before collection and no later than 6 am on collection day. Place bulk items where your trash and recycling are collected. A sanitation violation may be distributed to those who place items in a public space at any time other than collection appointment. Standard water heaters are typically large metal household appliances. They should be cool and empty of all hot water before disposal. - If you can take it yourself for disposal, you may bring a cool and empty hot water heater to DC's Fort Totten Transfer Station for bulk waste disposal (No cost to DC residents). - If you cannot take it yourself to the Fort Totten Transfer Station, call DC 311 to place an order for "bulk waste disposal" and ask for instructions on when and where to place the water heater for bulk waste collection by DC DPW (No cost to DC residents). - If you cannot remove the water heater from your home for disposal, call a professional mover or junk hauler to remove it and dispose of it for you (Not free, ask the mover or junk hauler for an estimate).
disposal_conditions.dropoff: Drop off at Fort Totten Transfer Station - Bulk, Trash, Recycling Drop off item for recycling. Do not place a large water heater in your trash can for regular weekly trash collection, these require a bulk waste collection order through DC 311 .

disposal_conditions.compost: Yard Waste DPW collects yard waste from residences that receive DPW’s trash and recycling collection services. Yard waste consists of any one or a combination of the following: fallen leaves, grass clippings, plant trimmings, weeds, bulbs, branches, twigs, pinecones, and uprooted plants. Yard waste can also include bundled branches and tree limbs of no more than 4 inches in diameter and 4-feet long and tied together with natural twine into bundle no longer than 4-foot long. DPW will not collect yard waste that includes branches and tree limbs greater than 4 inches in diameter or 4 feet in length, and stumps, dirt, stones, rocks, broken concrete, and broken pottery flowerpots. Yard waste DOES NOT include large tree limbs (>4 inches in diameter or >4 feet in length, stumps, dirt, stones, rocks, broken concrete, and broken pottery flower pots. Please visit our website for more information . Yard waste collection is now year-round. Residents can call 311 to make an appointment for yard waste collection. Yard waste collection is now year-round. Residents can call 311 to make an appointment for yard waste collection. Residents can also visit www.311.dc.gov to make an appointment or download and schedule an appointment using the 311 mobile app available at the Google Play and Apple App stores. Resident may also drop off their yard waste at the Fort Totten Transfer Station Tuesday – Friday 10:00 am -2:00 pm and Saturday 7:00 am - 2:00 pm DPW only collects yard waste in paper bags, which is unlike previous years when plastic bags were acceptable for collection. Beginning August 16, 2021, yard waste will only be collected in paper bags. DPW will collect up to 20 paper bags of yard waste from residences that receive DPW’s trash and recycling collection services.
